name: Jaylin Noel
headline: Year-two leverage, but the math still favors patience
body:
Noel's rookie tape is a study in efficiency without volume. He logged just a 26% snap share and ran only 189 routes, yet posted a 0.405 EPA per play and a 48.6% success rate on 35 targets, hauling in 74.3% with a single drop. His 1.54 yards per route run and 7.42 air yards before the catch say the production was real when the offense looked his way; the 6.3% target share says it rarely did.

The opportunity argument is straightforward: Christian Kirk is gone, opening a path to a bigger role, and Noel flashed when handed snaps. But Tank Dell's return clouds that runway, and the modest projection near 22 catches and 239 yards reflects it. With an ADP outside WR90, he's a dynasty stash on age and upside more than a redraft target. Monitor the depth chart; the talent is there, the volume isn't yet.
